NAME

Write all bytes to a file descriptor, handling partial writes.

Detailed Description

🔧 Shared cross-platform system utilities (safe string functions, etc.)

Definition in file system.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define BIN_SUFFIX ''

Definition at line 30 of file system.c.

#define PLATFORM_MAX_PATH_LENGTH 4096

Maximum path length supported by the operating system

Platform-specific values:

  • Windows: 32767 characters (extended-length path with \?\ prefix)
  • Linux: 4096 bytes (PATH_MAX from limits.h)
  • macOS: 1024 bytes (PATH_MAX from sys/syslimits.h)

Note: Windows legacy MAX_PATH (260) is too restrictive for modern use. We use the extended-length limit instead.

Definition at line 69 of file system.c.

bool check_binary_in_path_uncached (const char * bin_name) [extern]

Referenced by platform_is_binary_in_path().

size_t platform_write_all (int fd, const void * buf, size_t count)

Write all bytes to a file descriptor, handling partial writes. Handles partial writes and EAGAIN errors, retrying up to 1000 times before giving up. This ensures that data is fully written even when dealing with non-blocking I/O or interrupted syscalls.

Definition at line 225 of file system.c.

225                                                                  {
226   if (!buf || count == 0) {
227     return 0;
228   }
229 
230   size_t written_total = 0;
231   int attempts = 0;
232   const int MAX_ATTEMPTS = 1000;
233 
234   while (written_total < count && attempts < MAX_ATTEMPTS) {
235     ssize_t result = platform_write(fd, (const char *)buf + written_total, count - written_total);
236 
237     if (result > 0) {
238       written_total += (size_t)result;
239       attempts = 0; // Reset attempt counter on successful write
240     } else if (result < 0) {
241       // Handle EAGAIN (non-blocking would-block) with sleep instead of tight loop
242       if (errno == EAGAIN || errno == EWOULDBLOCK) {
243         // Sleep 100us before retrying to avoid busy-waiting and spinning CPU
244         platform_sleep_us(100);
245       } else {
246         // Other write errors - log and retry
247         log_warn("platform_write_all: write() error on fd=%d (wrote %zu/%zu so far, errno=%d)", fd, written_total,
248                  count, errno);
249       }
250       attempts++;
251     } else {
252       // result == 0: no bytes written, retry
253       attempts++;
254     }
255   }
256 
257   if (attempts >= MAX_ATTEMPTS && written_total < count) {
258     log_warn("platform_write_all: Hit retry limit on fd=%d: wrote %zu of %zu bytes", fd, written_total, count);
259   }
260 
261   return written_total;
262 }




References platform_sleep_us(), and
    platform_write().

int
  safe_fprintf (FILE * stream, const char * format, ...)


Safe formatted output to file stream.


Parameters


stream Output file stream


format Printf-style format string


Returns


Number of characters written, or -1 on error


Safely formats and prints output to a file stream. Returns the
    number of characters written, or -1 on error.


Definition at line 173 of file system.c.


173                                                         {
174   if (!stream || !format) {
175     return -1;
176   }
177 
178   va_list args;
179   va_start(args, format);
180   int ret = vfprintf(stream, format, args);
181   va_end(args);
182 
183   return ret;
184 }






References args.

    asciichat_print_error_context(), log_init(),

int
  safe_snprintf (char * buffer, size_t buffer_size, const char * format,
  ...)


Safe formatted string printing to buffer.


Parameters


buffer Output buffer


buffer_size Size of output buffer


format Printf-style format string


Returns


Number of characters written, or -1 on error


Safely formats a string into a buffer with bounds checking. Uses
    platform_snprintf for cross-platform implementation. Returns the number of
    characters written (not including null terminator). Returns -1 if buffer is
    too small.


Definition at line 149 of file system.c.


149                                                                              {
150   if (!buffer || !format || buffer_size == 0) {
151     return -1;
152   }
153 
154   va_list args;
155   va_start(args, format);
156 
157   /* Delegate to platform_snprintf for actual formatting */
158   int ret = platform_vsnprintf(buffer, buffer_size, format, args);
159 
160   va_end(args);
161   return ret;
162 }






References args, buffer_size, and
    platform_vsnprintf().

int
  safe_vsnprintf (char * buffer, size_t buffer_size, const char * format,
  va_list ap)


Safe formatted string printing with va_list.


Parameters


buffer Output buffer (can be NULL if buffer_size
  is 0 for size calculation)


buffer_size Size of output buffer


format Printf-style format string


ap Variable argument list


Returns


Number of characters written, or -1 on error


Safely formats a string into a buffer with bounds checking using
    va_list. Uses platform_vsnprintf for cross-platform implementation. Returns
    the number of characters written (not including null terminator). Special
    case: NULL buffer with size 0 returns required buffer size for size
    calculation. Returns -1 if buffer is too small, format is invalid, or buffer
    is NULL with size > 0.


Definition at line 200 of file system.c.


200                                                                                      {
201   if (!format) {
202     return -1;
203   }
204 
205   // Allow NULL buffer with size 0 for size calculation (standard vsnprintf behavior)
206   if (!buffer && buffer_size > 0) {
207     return -1; // Non-NULL buffer required when buffer_size > 0
208   }
209 
210   /* Delegate to platform_vsnprintf for actual formatting */
211   return platform_vsnprintf(buffer, buffer_size, format, ap);
212 }






References buffer_size, and
  platform_vsnprintf().


Referenced by disconnect_client_for_bad_data(),
    format_message(), log_file_msg(), log_mmap_write(),
    log_msg(), and log_plain_msg().
